commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

commit.message:
fix(build): remove JSX.Element return types to fix Vercel TS build

JSX namespace isn't available without explicit React import in this
tsconfig setup. Dropping the redundant explicit return types lets TS
infer them, unblocking the Vercel production build.

commit.message:
fix(build): exclude test files from tsc -b and restore JSX types

- Add src/vite-env.d.ts (standard Vite pattern) so vite/client types
  are available without overriding TypeScript's "types" field
- Remove "types": ["vite/client"] from tsconfig.app.json — this was
  blocking @types/react auto-discovery, causing JSX.Element errors
- Exclude *.test.ts/tsx from tsconfig.app.json so production build
  does not compile Vitest globals (vi, describe, expect)

Release v7.0.1: Switch dependencies to main branch (#57)

* chore: Bump version to 6.6.0 after release 6.5.0

Automated version bump following release 6.5.0.
Previous version: 6.5.0
New version: 6.6.0
Bump type: minor
Project type: swift-package

🤖 Generated by GitHub Actions

* fix(GuionDocument): Add PDF file marker handling to prevent UTF-8 decode errors

PDF files are binary and cannot be decoded as UTF-8 text. When a PDF file is
detected, return the special marker 'PDF_FILE' that FileDocumentView recognizes
and uses to invoke PDFScreenplayParser with the sourceFileURL directly.

This fixes the 'Could not decode file as UTF-8' error when opening PDF files.

* fix(GuionDocument): Write PDF data to temp file for secure parsing

PDF files cannot be decoded as UTF-8. When a PDF is detected:
1. Write the binary PDF data to a temp file in the app's temp directory
2. Return 'PDF_TEMP_FILE:/path/to/temp.pdf' format string
3. FileDocumentView extracts the path and uses it for PDFScreenplayParser

This approach ensures PDF data is securely accessible during parsing without
requiring sourceFileURL (which isn't available in FileDocument workflow).

* feat(PDFScreenplayParser): Add explicit Fountain format directive to AI prompt

Enhanced the Apple Intelligence prompt to explicitly state that output MUST be
in valid Fountain screenplay format with NO explanations, markdown, or code blocks.

This ensures the AI model outputs pure Fountain text that can be directly parsed
without requiring additional cleanup or format detection.

* feat: Add voice download tools and documentation audit

This commit includes comprehensive documentation reorganization and new voice download automation tools.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* debug: Add comprehensive Apple Intelligence debugging to PDF parser

Added detailed debug logging throughout PDF parsing workflow to diagnose Apple Intelligence availability issues:

**convertToFountain:**
- Log when FoundationModels available at compile time
- Log AI conversion success/failure
- Log full error details when falling back to basic conversion
- Log error type and description for diagnosis

**convertToFountainWithAI:**
- Log entry into AI conversion
- Log OS version and chip type (Apple Silicon vs Intel)
- Log SystemLanguageModel creation and availability check
- Log detailed reasons when model.isAvailable returns false
- Log AI request/response with character counts
- Log first 200 chars of AI output for verification
- Log any errors during AI session with full details

**Diagnostic Information:**
- FoundationModels compile-time availability
- SystemLanguageModel runtime availability
- OS version detection
- Apple Silicon detection (macOS)
- Model availability reasons
- Request/response sizes
- Error types and descriptions

This will help identify why Apple Intelligence may be unavailable:
- Not enabled in System Settings
- Device doesn't meet requirements (M1+/A17 Pro+)
- iOS version too old (need 26.2+) or macOS (need 26.0+)
- Language model not downloaded
- Session/request errors

* feat: Implement page-by-page AI conversion for PDFs

Refactored PDF parsing to process pages individually through Apple Intelligence
to avoid the 4,096 token context window limit.

**Key Changes:**
- extractPages() returns array of page texts instead of concatenated string
- convertPagesToFountain() processes each page individually with AI
- convertPageToFountainWithAI() handles single-page AI conversion
- convertPageToFountainBasic() provides per-page heuristic fallback
- convertAllPagesToFountainBasic() converts all pages with heuristics

**Benefits:**
- ✅ Handles screenplays of any size (no token limit errors)
- ✅ Each page processed individually (~500-1,000 tokens per page)
- ✅ Per-page progress tracking ("Converting page X of Y")
- ✅ Graceful degradation: individual page failures use heuristics
- ✅ Smart fallback: if >50% pages fail AI, re-process all with heuristics
- ✅ Better UX with detailed page-by-page progress updates

**Error Handling:**
- Individual page AI failures don't stop conversion (use heuristics)
- High failure rate (>50%) triggers full heuristic re-conversion
- Detailed debug logging per page with token estimates
- Success/failure statistics logged after completion

**Example:**
120-page screenplay:
- Old: 39,758 tokens → exceeds 4,096 limit → falls back entirely
- New: 120 pages × ~320 tokens each → all pages use AI ✅

Tested with "The Banshees of Inisherin" (120 pages, 115K chars).

* fix: Add iOS 26.2 availability check to testUnavailableAI

The testUnavailableAI test calls generateCastList(progress:) which requires
iOS 26.2+, but the test was missing the @available attribute.

This was causing iOS Unit Tests to fail on CI with:
error: 'generateCastList(progress:)' is only available in iOS 26.2 or newer

* feat(PDF): Extract attributed strings for richer AI parsing context

Extract font, style, and formatting information from PDF attributed strings
to provide Apple Intelligence with better context for screenplay element recognition.

Changes:
- Added PDFPageContent struct with plainText and formattedText
- Extract attributed strings from PDFPage (NSFont/UIFont)
- Detect BOLD, ITALIC, LARGE fonts, and CAPS text
- Annotate formatted text with style markers ([BOLD], [CAPS], etc.)
- Updated AI prompt to use formatting cues for element identification
- All conversion methods now use PDFPageContent instead of plain strings

Benefits:
- AI can distinguish scene headings (BOLD+CAPS) from dialogue
- Character names easier to identify (CAPS, short lines)
- Formatting preserved from original PDF layout
- Better recognition for non-standard screenplay PDFs
- Falls back to plain text if attributed string unavailable

Platform support:
- macOS: Uses NSFont and AppKit
- iOS: Uses UIFont and UIKit

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(pdf): Fix progress bar not advancing during page-by-page parsing

Fixed integer division bug that prevented progress bar from advancing
smoothly during PDF page-by-page AI conversion.

The bug:
- Progress calculation used integer division: (60 * pageNum) / totalPages
- For most pages, this rounded down to 0 or very small increments
- Example: Page 1 of 100 = (60 * 1) / 100 = 0 (integer division!)

The fix:
- Use floating point division then convert to Int64
- Progress now advances smoothly from 20% to 80% during conversion
- Affects both AI conversion path (line 364) and fallback path (line 771)

Before: Progress bar stayed at 20% until final page
After: Progress bar smoothly advances for each page processed

commit.message:
Fix AI turns not firing after human ends turn

Replace two-effect approach (roll dice effect + build effect) with a
single effect that chains timeouts in one closure. The previous approach
had a coordination bug: the step-2 effect depended on game.dice changing
across render cycles, which failed to trigger reliably. The new approach
uses a cancelled flag (handles React Strict Mode double-invocation) and
nested setTimeout so roll → build happen sequentially in one closure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix OP-20 payment verification race condition on production

The backend's verifyOp20Payment() failed on Railway because getTransaction()
can't find freshly broadcast transactions still in the mempool. Added retry
logic (3 attempts, 2.5s apart) with trust-mode fallback matching the existing
native BTC verification pattern. Entry is recorded with isVerified=false but
the player can start playing immediately.

commit.message:
fix(favs): merge all content into single FlatList to eliminate phantom scroll gap

The blank space was caused by having the grid in a separate ScrollView
below fixed siblings (search bar + filter pills). When the native
UIScrollView preserved a stale contentOffset, there was no reliable
way to detect and reset it.

Fix: make the FlatList own the ENTIRE scroll area. Search bar, filter
pills, and filterCount bar move into ListHeaderComponent. This mirrors
the architecture of the Discover tab (which has no gap issue) and makes
phantom gaps structurally impossible — there is no seam between header
and grid because they are all inside the same scroll container.

Also: replace ScrollView ref with FlatList ref for scrollToOffset.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  safe PostgREST null filters, user_id membership pre-check, new ok/code contract
- accept-invite: distinct ALREADY_ACCEPTED (409) / EXPIRED_INVITE (410) / NOT_FOUND (404),
  standardised { ok, code } responses, dynamic CORS per request
- resend-invite (new): fetch by id, email first, extend expires_at only after confirmed send
- revoke-invite (new): fetch by id, authorize owner/admin, DELETE pending invite row
- list-pending-invites (new): service role key bypasses owner-only RLS on invite_tokens
- PendingInvitesList (new): per-row Resend/Revoke with loading state
- WorkspaceSettings: pending invites query via Edge Function, PendingInvitesList in Users tab
- InviteMemberDialog: three-layer response guards, per-email ok/code handling

commit.message:
prod: comprehensive production hardening — security, resilience, CI/CD

Security:
- IAM: Scope bedrock:InvokeModel* from Resource:"*" to specific ARN patterns
- Auth: VSMFunctionUrl AuthType:NONE → AWS_IAM, remove Principal:"*"
- Error handling: Convert all 22 bare except: to except Exception with logging
- Remove hardcoded localhost fallbacks (segment_runner, python_bridge)
- Document states:SendTask* Resource:"*" as by-design (not ARN-addressable)

Resilience:
- Lambda timeout watchdog: 15s-buffer deadline via context.get_remaining_time_in_millis()
- 2PC Phase 2 retry: exponential backoff (3 attempts) for transient DynamoDB errors
- Segment-level idempotency: DDB dedup with S3 offload for >350KB results
- ThreadPoolExecutor: deadline-aware timeout for aggregator futures

CI/CD & Infrastructure:
- Add pytest gate to backend-deploy.yml (runs before SAM deploy)
- Add 18 unit tests: hash_utils (8), kernel_protocol (4), speculative_controller (6)
- S3 hardening: AES256 encryption, versioning, PublicAccessBlockConfiguration
- DynamoDB PITR: Enable on 6 critical tables (executions, workflows, users, manifests, audit, tokens)
- Pin critical dependency versions (boto3>=1.34.0 for Bedrock Inference Profiles)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(security): remove tracked legacy archive containing PII from public repo

The archive/legacy_on_premise_dw/ directory contained 132 tracked files
including a SQL view with real personal data (full name, date of birth,
height) and SSIS packages with Windows domain credentials.

The .gitignore rule `legacy_on_premise_dw/` only matched root-level paths,
not the `archive/` subdirectory. Fixed by adding explicit rule.

Files remain in git history but are removed from HEAD.

fix: nixpacks.toml - don't override install phase, only add build step

Previous version overrode [phases.install] which broke nixpacks' automatic
pip install (pip not in PATH at that stage). Now uses [phases.build] to
run playwright install chromium after nixpacks handles pip automatically.

repository.name: screenpub
repository.description: Convert Screenplay in PDF form to epub

commit.message:
feat: detect and render screenplay page headers (date / title / page)

Page headers (e.g. '1/24/13  FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT  3.') are now
detected by scanning the top 90pt of each page for lines that
contain a recognisable draft label (FINAL/REVISED/SHOOTING SCRIPT
etc.). The matching lines are removed from the main content stream
and re-inserted immediately after each page-break marker as a
structured three-column flex row:

  date (left) | draft title (centre, italic) | page number (right)

Implementation:
- RawLine gains a y0 field (bottom of line in PDF points)
- extract_lines() now returns (lines, page_heights)
- build_page_headers() groups top-zone lines by y-row, identifies
  header rows containing a DRAFT_LABEL_RE match, stores them keyed
  by page number with \x00-delimited parts (date/title/num)
- merge_elements() accepts page_headers and inserts a page_header
  ScriptElement right after each page-break element
- elements_to_html_chapters() renders page_header as a flex <div>
- calibrate() now receives filtered_lines (headers excluded) so
  centred draft titles don't skew column detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement Canny + HoughLinesP sheet counting

- Grayscale → GaussianBlur → Canny → HoughLinesP pipeline
- Filter near-horizontal lines (±15°) and cluster by Y-position
- Overlay detected lines + cluster index labels on original image
- Toggle between original+overlay and edge-detection views
- Parameter panel: blur kernel, Canny low/high, min line length, cluster tolerance, max gap

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
